introduction: This spec defines the `afc` CLI binary for the af-hub platform, providing operator-driven access to login, API key management, workspace registration, and workspace token management via a persistent TOML configuration file. Note: requirements referencing external library APIs (github.com/spf13/cobra, github.com/BurntSushi/toml, github.com/pkg/browser) are based on PRD descriptions and should be cross-checked against the installed library versions before implementation.

glossary.$HOME/.af/: The configuration directory for the afc CLI, created with mode 0700 on first run.
glossary.$HOME/.af/config.toml: The persistent TOML configuration file storing hub connection and authentication state for the afc CLI.
glossary.--api-key: CLI flag that overrides AF_HUB_API_KEY and the api_key config file value.
glossary.--expires: CLI flag accepted by afc login and afc workspace token create; restricts expiry to {0, 30, 60, 90} days.
glossary.--hub-url: CLI flag that overrides AF_HUB_URL and the hub_url config file value.
glossary.--provider: CLI flag for afc login specifying the OAuth provider (e.g., 'github').
glossary.--user-id: CLI flag that overrides AF_HUB_USER_ID and the user_id config file value.
glossary.-ldflags: Go build flag mechanism used to inject the version string into the afc binary at build time.
glossary.AF_HUB_API_KEY: Environment variable that overrides the api_key config file value.
glossary.AF_HUB_URL: Environment variable that overrides the hub_url config file value.
glossary.AF_HUB_USER_ID: Environment variable that overrides the user_id config file value.
glossary.Authorization: Bearer: HTTP header scheme used for all authenticated API requests, with the api_key as the token value.
glossary.CSRF state parameter: A cryptographically random value embedded in the OAuth authorization URL and validated on callback to prevent cross-site request forgery.
glossary.DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id: Hub API endpoint that revokes the API key identified by key_id.
glossary.DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:: Hub API endpoint that revokes a workspace token.
glossary.ExactArgs(1): Cobra validator that requires exactly one positional argument; used by afc workspace token revoke.
glossary.GET /api/v1/auth/providers: Hub API endpoint that returns the list of supported OAuth providers.
glossary.GET /api/v1/keys: Hub API endpoint that lists all API keys for the authenticated user.
glossary.GET /api/v1/teams: Hub API endpoint that lists all teams; used to resolve a team slug to a UUID.
glossary.GET /api/v1/workspaces: Hub API endpoint that lists all workspaces.
glossary.GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug: Hub API endpoint that retrieves a workspace by its slug.
glossary.GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens: Hub API endpoint that lists workspace tokens (metadata only).
glossary.POST /api/v1/auth/callback: Hub API endpoint that exchanges an OAuth authorization code for a user record and API key.
glossary.POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh: Hub API endpoint that rotates an existing API key identified by key_id.
glossary.POST /api/v1/workspaces: Hub API endpoint that creates a new workspace.
glossary.POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens: Hub API endpoint that creates a workspace token.
glossary.af-hub: The self-hosted hub server platform whose REST API this CLI consumes.
glossary.afc: The CLI binary produced by this spec; the primary non-browser interface for the af-hub platform.
glossary.api_key: Config file field storing the secret API key value used for Bearer authentication.
glossary.branch: Optional workspace creation payload field specifying the git branch or ref.
glossary.git_url: Workspace creation payload field containing the git repository URL.
glossary.github.com/BurntSushi/toml: Go library used to parse and encode the TOML configuration file.
glossary.github.com/pkg/browser: Go library used to open the OAuth authorization URL in the user's default browser.
glossary.github.com/spf13/cobra: Go library providing the CLI framework used to build afc commands.
glossary.hub_url: Config file field storing the base URL of the af-hub server.
glossary.key_id: Config file field storing the UUID of the current API key, used by keys refresh and keys revoke commands.
glossary.label: Optional workspace token creation payload field for a human-readable token label.
glossary.make build: Build command that compiles the afc binary to the bin/ directory with version injection.
glossary.os.CreateTemp: Go standard library function used to create temporary files for atomic config writes.
glossary.os.Rename: Go standard library function used to atomically replace the config file with the temp file.
glossary.redirect_uri: The OAuth callback URL sent to the hub server; format is http://localhost:<port>/callback.
glossary.slug: Workspace creation payload field and workspace identifier used in URL paths.
glossary.team_id: Workspace creation payload field containing the UUID of the team, resolved from a team slug.
glossary.user_id: Config file field storing the UUID of the authenticated user.
glossary./callback: API endpoint path or route segment.
glossary.0.1.0: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.<api_key>: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.<token-id>: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.

Code reference or literal value used in requirements.

glossary.Error: failed to parse config file: <par: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.

Code reference or literal value used in requirements.

user_story.goal: have the CLI automatically create a configuration directory and file on first run
user_story.benefit: I do not need to manually set up the config before using the CLI

acceptance_criteria.action: stores persistent configuration in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` with four fields: `hub_url`, `user_id`, `api_key`, and `key_id`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Config file at `$HOME/.af/config.toml` containing valid TOML with all four fields

acceptance_criteria.action: creates `$HOME/.af/` directory with mode 0700 and `$HOME/.af/config.toml` file with mode 0600 containing empty field values
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Directory and file created; CLI proceeds normally
acceptance_criteria.trigger: any command is invoked and `$HOME/.af/config.toml` does not exist

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `Error: failed to parse config file: <parse error>` to stderr and exits with code 1 without modifying or overwriting the file
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; config file unchanged

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive error to stderr and exits with code 1
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; no partial state left
edge_cases.error_condition: `$HOME/.af/` directory cannot be created due to filesystem permissions

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive error to stderr and exits with code 1
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1
edge_cases.error_condition: `$HOME/.af/config.toml` exists but is a directory rather than a file

user_story.goal: override config file values using environment variables or CLI flags
user_story.benefit: I can run one-off commands against a different hub without editing the config file

acceptance_criteria.action: resolves `hub_url`, `user_id`, and `api_key` with precedence: `--hub-url` / `--user-id` / `--api-key` CLI flags > `AF_HUB_URL` / `AF_HUB_USER_ID` / `AF_HUB_API_KEY` environment variables > `$HOME/.af/config.toml` fields > error
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Resolved value from highest-priority source; error message and exit code 1 if no source provides a required value

acceptance_criteria.action: resolves `key_id` exclusively from `$HOME/.af/config.toml`; no CLI flag or environment variable override exists for `key_id`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Value from config file, or error if absent

acceptance_criteria.action: prints a descriptive error to stderr specifying which value is missing and how to set it (via flag, env var, or config file field), then exits with code 1
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1 with descriptive error on stderr
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: a required configuration value is not available from any source (flag, env var, or config file)

edge_cases.action: uses the value from `AF_HUB_URL`, ignoring the config file value
edge_cases.return_contract: Effective hub_url equals the AF_HUB_URL value
edge_cases.trigger: a command requiring `hub_url` is invoked without `--hub-url` flag
edge_cases.condition: both `AF_HUB_URL` environment variable and `hub_url` config file field are set

edge_cases.action: uses the `--hub-url` flag value, ignoring both `AF_HUB_URL` and the config file
edge_cases.return_contract: Effective hub_url equals the --hub-url flag value
edge_cases.condition: `--hub-url` flag is provided alongside `AF_HUB_URL` and a config file value

user_story.goal: have config mutations written atomically so a crash mid-write does not corrupt my config
user_story.benefit: my config file is never left in a partially-written state

acceptance_criteria.action: writes config mutations by creating a temporary file named `config.toml.<random-suffix>` in `$HOME/.af/` using `os.CreateTemp`, writing the complete new config, then calling `os.Rename` to atomically replace `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Final `$HOME/.af/config.toml` contains the new config; no intermediate partial file persists

acceptance_criteria.action: creates temporary config files with mode 0600 matching the target config file permissions
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Temporary file and final config file both have mode 0600

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive error to stderr, exits with code 1, and leaves the existing `$HOME/.af/config.toml` unchanged
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; original config file unmodified
edge_cases.error_condition: `os.CreateTemp` fails when creating the temporary config file

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive error to stderr, exits with code 1, and leaves the temporary file in `$HOME/.af/` for manual cleanup; the original config is unchanged
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; original config file unmodified; temp file may remain
edge_cases.error_condition: `os.Rename` fails after the temporary file is written

user_story.goal: invoke afc with no arguments to see help, and use --version to confirm the installed version
user_story.benefit: I can quickly discover available commands and verify the binary version

acceptance_criteria.action: prints help text to stdout and exits with code 0 (Cobra default behavior)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; help text on stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `afc version <semver>` to stdout and exits with code 0; the version string is injected at build time via `-ldflags`; initial version is `0.1.0`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; string `afc version 0.1.0` (or current build version) on stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: supports `--help` and `afc help <command>` for all commands via Cobra built-in behavior
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; command help text on stdout

requirements.title: Flag validation for --expires and required string flags

user_story.goal: receive an immediate, clear error when I provide an invalid flag value
user_story.benefit: I do not waste time waiting for a network round-trip only to discover my input was invalid

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `Error: --expires must be one of: 0, 30, 60, 90` to stderr and exits with code 1 before making any network request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; no network request issued
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `--expires` is provided with a value not in {0, 30, 60, 90} for `afc login` or `afc workspace token create`

acceptance_criteria.action: prints a descriptive error to stderr and exits with code 1 before making any network request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; no network request issued
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `--git-url` or `--slug` is provided as an empty string for `afc workspace create`

acceptance_criteria.action: prints a descriptive error to stderr and exits with code 1 before making any network request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; no network request issued
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `--workspace` is provided as an empty string for workspace token commands

edge_cases.action: defers all format, URL validity, slug character, and label length validation for `--git-url`, `--slug`, `--branch`, and `--label` to the server; the CLI only checks for non-empty string values
edge_cases.return_contract: Server error response forwarded to stderr; exit code 1

requirements.title: Login command with OAuth authorization code flow

user_story.goal: authenticate with the hub using my OAuth provider account
user_story.benefit: I obtain stored credentials that subsequent CLI commands use automatically

acceptance_criteria.action: fetches the provider list from `GET /api/v1/auth/providers`, validates that the `--provider` value is present in the returned list, generates a cryptographically random CSRF state parameter of at least 16 bytes of entropy, starts a local HTTP callback server on a random OS-assigned port with callback path `/callback`, and opens the authorization URL in the user's default browser via `github.com/pkg/browser`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Browser opens authorization URL; callback server is listening; full authorization URL is printed to stderr

acceptance_criteria.action: always prints the full authorization URL to stderr regardless of whether the browser opened successfully, enabling manual use in headless or restricted environments
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Authorization URL appears on stderr

acceptance_criteria.action: validates the state parameter, captures the authorization code, responds to the browser with HTTP 200 and an HTML success page containing a visible success message (e.g., `Login successful! You may close this tab.`), then exchanges the code with `POST /api/v1/auth/callback`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Browser receives HTTP 200 with HTML success page; code exchange request sent to hub

acceptance_criteria.action: stores `hub_url`, `user_id` (from `response.user.id`), `api_key` (from `response.api_key.key`), and `key_id` (from `response.api_key.id`) in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` via atomic write; default `--provider` is `github`; default `--expires` is 90
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; config file updated with new credentials

acceptance_criteria.action: sends `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` with JSON payload containing `provider`, `code`, `redirect_uri`, and `expires` as an integer (0 for no expiry, or 30/60/90 for days)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Request payload conforms to schema; `expires` is always an integer

acceptance_criteria.action: silently overwrites the existing credentials with the new ones; the old `api_key` is not automatically revoked
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; config updated with new credentials; old key remains active on server

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `Error: unsupported provider: <value>. Available: <comma-separated list>` to stderr and exits with code 1
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; no OAuth flow initiated
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `--provider` value is not present in the list returned by `GET /api/v1/auth/providers`

edge_cases.action: shuts down the local callback server, prints an error to stderr, and exits with code 1; no partial credentials are written
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; callback server stopped; config unchanged
edge_cases.error_condition: 2 minutes elapse without receiving an OAuth callback

edge_cases.action: continues the flow normally; the authorization URL already printed to stderr allows the user to open it manually
edge_cases.return_contract: Login flow continues; authorization URL on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: `github.com/pkg/browser` fails to open the authorization URL

edge_cases.action: ignores the callback, prints an error to stderr, and exits with code 1; no credentials are stored
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; config unchanged
edge_cases.error_condition: OAuth callback contains a state parameter that does not match the generated CSRF state

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive error to stderr and exits with code 1 before opening the browser
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; no browser opened
edge_cases.error_condition: local callback server port binding fails (OS cannot assign a port)

edge_cases.action: surfaces the error to stderr and exits with code 1 before initiating any OAuth flow
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; no OAuth flow initiated
edge_cases.error_condition: `GET /api/v1/auth/providers` returns a network error or non-2xx response

user_story.benefit: I can audit which keys are active and identify keys to revoke

acceptance_criteria.action: sends `GET /api/v1/keys` with `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` header and prints the raw response body to stdout, pretty-printed with 2-space indentation
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; JSON response body on stdout with 2-space indentation

edge_cases.action: attempts to parse the response body as JSON and prints the error message to stderr, or prints `Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP <status>).` if the body is not valid JSON, then exits with code 1
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; error on stderr

user_story.benefit: I can replace a potentially compromised key without losing my session

acceptance_criteria.action: sends `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` using `key_id` from `$HOME/.af/config.toml`, then on success updates both `api_key` and `key_id` in config via atomic write with the values from the response, and prints the response body to stdout pretty-printed with 2-space indentation
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; updated credentials in config; new key JSON on stdout
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `afc keys refresh` is invoked and `key_id` is present in config

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `Error: key_id is not set. Run "afc login" first.` to stderr and exits with code 1 without making any network request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; no network request; config unchanged

edge_cases.action: prints the error message from the JSON response body to stderr, or `Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP <status>).` if non-JSON, and exits with code 1 without modifying config
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; config unchanged

user_story.goal: revoke my current API key and clear local credentials
user_story.benefit: I can disconnect from the hub and ensure the key cannot be reused

acceptance_criteria.action: clears `api_key`, `key_id`, and `user_id` in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` via atomic write, and prints `API key revoked.` to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; `api_key`, `key_id`, `user_id` cleared in config; message on stderr
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `afc keys revoke` is invoked and the server returns a 2xx response

acceptance_criteria.action: clears `api_key`, `key_id`, and `user_id` in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` via atomic write, and prints `API key not found on server. Local credentials cleared.` to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; credentials cleared in config; message on stderr
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `afc keys revoke` is invoked and `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` returns 404

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `Error: key_id is not set. Run "afc login" first.` to stderr and exits with code 1 without making any network request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; no network request; config unchanged

acceptance_criteria.action: prints the error message from the JSON response body to stderr, or `Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP <status>).` if non-JSON, exits with code 1, and does not modify config
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; config unchanged
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` returns a non-2xx response other than 404

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive error about the config write failure to stderr and exits with code 1; the key is revoked on the server but local config may retain stale values
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; warning on stderr; config may not be cleared
edge_cases.error_condition: atomic config write fails after a successful server revocation

user_story.goal: register a new workspace with the hub
user_story.benefit: I can associate a git repository with the hub for CI/CD integration

acceptance_criteria.action: if `--team` is provided, resolves the team slug to a UUID via `GET /api/v1/teams` by searching the slug field; then sends `POST /api/v1/workspaces` with payload containing `git_url`, `slug`, and optionally `branch` and `team_id`; prints the response body to stdout pretty-printed with 2-space indentation
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; workspace JSON on stdout with 2-space indentation

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `team not found: <slug>` to stderr and exits with code 1 without sending `POST /api/v1/workspaces`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; no workspace creation request
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: team slug resolution via `GET /api/v1/teams` yields zero matching teams

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `ambiguous team slug: <slug>` to stderr and exits with code 1 without sending `POST /api/v1/workspaces`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; no workspace creation request
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: team slug resolution via `GET /api/v1/teams` yields multiple matching teams

acceptance_criteria.action: omits `branch` from the `POST /api/v1/workspaces` payload when `--branch` is not provided, and omits `team_id` when `--team` is not provided
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Payload contains only the fields with provided values

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive network error to stderr and exits with code 1 without sending `POST /api/v1/workspaces`
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; no workspace creation request
edge_cases.error_condition: `GET /api/v1/teams` request fails with a network error during team slug resolution

edge_cases.action: prints `Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP <status>).` to stderr and exits with code 1
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; error on stderr without raw HTML body
edge_cases.error_condition: `POST /api/v1/workspaces` returns a non-2xx response with a non-JSON body (e.g., HTML 502)

user_story.goal: list all workspaces or retrieve a specific workspace by slug
user_story.benefit: I can inspect workspace state and configuration without accessing the hub web interface

acceptance_criteria.action: sends `GET /api/v1/workspaces` with `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` header and prints the raw response body to stdout pretty-printed with 2-space indentation
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; workspaces JSON array on stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: sends `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug` with `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` header and prints the raw response body to stdout pretty-printed with 2-space indentation
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; workspace JSON object on stdout

edge_cases.action: prints the JSON error message from the response body to stderr and exits with code 1
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; error message on stderr

user_story.goal: create a workspace token for programmatic access
user_story.benefit: I can grant CI/CD systems access to a workspace without sharing my personal API key

acceptance_criteria.action: sends `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens` with JSON payload containing `expires` as an integer (default 30) and optionally `label` if provided; prints the response body (including full token value) to stdout pretty-printed with 2-space indentation; does not store the token in `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; token JSON (including secret value) on stdout; config unchanged

acceptance_criteria.action: omits `label` from the `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens` payload when `--label` is not provided; always includes `expires` as an integer
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Payload omits label field when not provided; expires always present as integer

edge_cases.action: prints the JSON error message to stderr, or `Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP <status>).` if non-JSON, and exits with code 1
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; error on stderr; no token stored

user_story.goal: list all tokens for a workspace
user_story.benefit: I can audit active tokens and identify tokens to revoke

acceptance_criteria.action: sends `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens` with `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` header and prints the raw response body to stdout pretty-printed with 2-space indentation
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; token metadata JSON array on stdout (no secrets)

user_story.goal: revoke a specific workspace token by its ID
user_story.benefit: I can immediately invalidate a compromised or unused token

acceptance_criteria.action: sends `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:token_id` and prints `Token <token-id> revoked.` to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0; `Token <token-id> revoked.` on stderr
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `afc workspace token revoke --workspace <slug> <token-id>` is invoked and the server returns 2xx

acceptance_criteria.action: Cobra's `ExactArgs(1)` validator prints a usage error to stderr and exits with code 1 before any network request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; Cobra usage error on stderr

edge_cases.action: prints the JSON error message to stderr, or `Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP <status>).` if non-JSON, and exits with code 1
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; error on stderr

user_story.goal: receive machine-parseable JSON on stdout and human-readable messages on stderr
user_story.benefit: I can pipe command output to other tools without filtering out status messages

acceptance_criteria.action: prints all raw API response bodies to stdout pretty-printed with 2-space indentation, without reshaping, filtering, or transforming any fields
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stdout contains only JSON data; stderr contains only human-readable messages

acceptance_criteria.action: writes all human-readable status messages and error messages to stderr, never to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stderr contains status/error messages; stdout contains only JSON or is empty

user_story.goal: have all API requests automatically authenticated with my stored API key
user_story.benefit: I do not need to manually pass credentials with every command

acceptance_criteria.action: includes the HTTP header `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` on all authenticated API requests, where `<api_key>` is the resolved API key value
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Every authenticated request carries the Authorization header

acceptance_criteria.action: uses a fixed HTTP client timeout of 30 seconds for all outbound requests
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Requests that exceed 30 seconds produce a timeout error on stderr and exit code 1

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive timeout error to stderr and exits with code 1; no retry is attempted
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; timeout error on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: an outbound HTTP request times out (30-second deadline exceeded)

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive network error to stderr and exits with code 1; no retry is attempted
edge_cases.return_contract: Exit code 1; descriptive network error on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: an outbound HTTP request fails due to a network error (e.g., connection refused)

user_story.goal: receive a clean error message when the server returns an unexpected non-JSON response
user_story.benefit: I am not presented with raw HTML or binary content when a proxy or load balancer intercepts the request

acceptance_criteria.action: prints `Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP <status>).` to stderr without including the raw body, then exits with code 1
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 1; clean error message on stderr; raw body not printed
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: a non-2xx response body cannot be parsed as JSON

correctness_properties.title: Config resolution precedence is strictly ordered
correctness_properties.for_any: configuration value with sources from `--hub-url`/`--user-id`/`--api-key` flags, `AF_HUB_URL`/`AF_HUB_USER_ID`/`AF_HUB_API_KEY` env vars, and `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
correctness_properties.invariant: the resolved value always comes from the highest-priority source present; a flag value always overrides an env var, which always overrides a config file value

correctness_properties.title: Atomic write never leaves a partial config
correctness_properties.for_any: config mutation operation (login, keys refresh, keys revoke)
correctness_properties.invariant: `$HOME/.af/config.toml` is either fully updated or entirely unchanged; a partial write caused by a crash between temp file creation and rename does not corrupt the config

correctness_properties.title: CSRF state uniqueness across login invocations
correctness_properties.invariant: the generated CSRF state parameters are distinct with overwhelming probability; each state contains at least 16 bytes of cryptographically random entropy

correctness_properties.title: Workspace token value is never persisted to config
correctness_properties.invariant: the token secret value returned by the server is printed to stdout and never written to `$HOME/.af/config.toml`

correctness_properties.title: stdout contains only JSON; stderr contains only human messages
correctness_properties.invariant: stdout receives only raw API response JSON (or is empty); stderr receives only human-readable status messages, error messages, and the authorization URL; the two streams never receive each other's content

correctness_properties.title: --expires values are always validated before network contact
correctness_properties.for_any: invocation of `afc login` or `afc workspace token create` with `--expires`
correctness_properties.invariant: if `--expires` is not in {0, 30, 60, 90}, the CLI exits with code 1 and the error message before issuing any HTTP request

correctness_properties.title: Non-JSON error bodies never produce raw HTML on stderr
correctness_properties.for_any: non-2xx HTTP response whose body is not parseable as JSON
correctness_properties.invariant: the CLI prints only `Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP <status>).` to stderr and never prints the raw response body

execution_paths.title: Full end-to-end login and workspace creation flow

steps.action: runs `afc login --provider github --expires 90`
reads `$HOME/.af/config.toml` (creates it if absent); fetches `GET /api/v1/auth/providers` from hub server (spec 1 server_foundation routing); validates provider is in list

generates a cryptographically random CSRF state parameter; binds a local HTTP callback server on OS-assigned port; opens authorization URL in browser via `github.com/pkg/browser`; prints authorization URL to stderr
redirects browser to `http://localhost:<port>/callback?code=<code>&state=<state>`
validates state parameter; captures authorization code; responds with HTTP 200 HTML success page to browser
sends `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` to hub (spec 2 oauth_and_users handler) with provider, code, redirect_uri, and expires=90
validates code with OAuth provider; creates or retrieves user; issues API key; returns user.id, api_key.id, api_key.key in response
writes `hub_url`, `user_id`, `api_key`, `key_id` to `$HOME/.af/config.toml` via atomic write (os.CreateTemp + os.Rename); exits with code 0
runs `afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-workspace --team my-team`
resolves config values; sends `GET /api/v1/teams` to hub (spec 3 teams handler) with Bearer auth; finds team UUID matching slug `my-team`
returns list of teams including the matching team with its UUID
sends `POST /api/v1/workspaces` to hub (spec 4 workspaces_and_tokens handler) with git_url, slug, and team_id; prints response JSON to stdout with 2-space indentation; exits with code 0
creates workspace record; returns workspace JSON
runs `afc keys refresh` after a successful login
reads `key_id` from `$HOME/.af/config.toml`; validates it is non-empty
sends `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` with `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` header
rotates the API key; returns new api_key.id and api_key.key in response
updates `api_key` and `key_id` in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` via atomic write; prints new key JSON to stdout with 2-space indentation; exits with code 0
runs `afc login --provider github` but does not complete the browser flow
starts local callback server; opens browser; prints authorization URL to stderr
waits up to 2 minutes for a callback; 2-minute timer fires with no callback received
shuts down the callback server; prints timeout error to stderr; exits with code 1 without modifying `$HOME/.af/config.toml`
runs `afc workspace token revoke --workspace my-workspace abc-token-id`
validates `--workspace` is non-empty; confirms `<token-id>` argument is present via ExactArgs(1)
sends `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/my-workspace/tokens/abc-token-id` with `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` header to hub (spec 4 workspaces_and_tokens handler)
revokes the token; returns 2xx response
prints `Token abc-token-id revoked.` to stderr; exits with code 0
runs `afc keys revoke` when the key was already revoked on the server
reads `key_id` from `$HOME/.af/config.toml`; validates non-empty
sends `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` with `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` header
returns 404 (key not found)
treats 404 as success; clears `api_key`, `key_id`, `user_id` in `$HOME/.af/config.toml` via atomic write; prints `API key not found on server. Local credentials cleared.` to stderr; exits with code 0

error_handling.condition: Config file exists but contains unparseable TOML
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints `Error: failed to parse config file: <parse error>` to stderr and exits with code 1; config file is not modified or overwritten

error_handling.condition: Required config value (hub_url, user_id, api_key) is absent from all sources
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints a descriptive error naming the missing value and how to set it (flag, env var, or config field), then exits with code 1

error_handling.condition: `key_id` is absent or empty in config for keys refresh or keys revoke
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints `Error: key_id is not set. Run "afc login" first.` to stderr and exits with code 1 without any network request
CLI prints `Error: --expires must be one of: 0, 30, 60, 90` to stderr and exits with code 1 before any network request

error_handling.condition: `--provider` value is not in the list from `GET /api/v1/auth/providers`
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints `Error: unsupported provider: <value>. Available: <comma-separated list>` to stderr and exits with code 1

error_handling.condition: OAuth callback 2-minute timeout elapses without receiving a callback
error_handling.behavior: CLI shuts down the local callback server, prints a timeout error to stderr, and exits with code 1 without writing to config

error_handling.condition: OAuth callback state parameter does not match generated CSRF state
error_handling.behavior: CLI ignores the callback, prints an error to stderr, and exits with code 1 without storing credentials

error_handling.condition: Non-2xx HTTP response body cannot be parsed as JSON
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints `Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP <status>).` to stderr without the raw body and exits with code 1

error_handling.condition: Network error (connection refused, timeout) on any outbound HTTP request
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints a descriptive error to stderr and exits with code 1; no retry is attempted

error_handling.condition: Team slug resolution returns zero matches
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints `team not found: <slug>` to stderr and exits with code 1 without sending the workspace creation request

error_handling.condition: Team slug resolution returns multiple matches
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints `ambiguous team slug: <slug>` to stderr and exits with code 1 without sending the workspace creation request

error_handling.condition: `afc workspace token revoke` is invoked without the `<token-id>` positional argument
error_handling.behavior: Cobra's ExactArgs(1) validator prints a usage error to stderr and exits with code 1; no network request is made

error_handling.condition: `os.CreateTemp` fails when creating the temporary config file during an atomic write
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints a descriptive error to stderr, exits with code 1, and leaves the existing config file unchanged

error_handling.condition: `os.Rename` fails after the temp config file is written
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints a descriptive error to stderr, exits with code 1; temp file may remain in `$HOME/.af/`; original config is unchanged
CLI treats this as a success scenario: clears api_key, key_id, and user_id from config and prints `API key not found on server. Local credentials cleared.` to stderr; exits with code 0

error_handling.condition: Local callback server port binding fails during `afc login`
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints a descriptive error to stderr and exits with code 1 before opening the browser

symbols.name: Command
symbols.signature: type Command struct { Use string; Short string; Long string; Version string; RunE func(cmd *Command, args []string) error; ... }
symbols.notes: Primary struct for defining CLI commands; Version field used for --version flag; RunE preferred over Run for error propagation without process termination from library code

symbols.name: ExactArgs
symbols.notes: Used by afc workspace token revoke to require exactly one positional token-id argument

symbols.name: Command.PersistentFlags
symbols.notes: Used to register --hub-url, --user-id, --api-key flags on the root command so they are available to all subcommands

symbols.name: Decode
symbols.signature: func Decode(data string, v interface{}) (MetaData, error)
symbols.notes: Used to parse config.toml contents into the config struct; error indicates malformed TOML

symbols.name: NewEncoder
symbols.notes: Used to encode the config struct to TOML for atomic writes

symbols.name: OpenURL
symbols.notes: Used to open the OAuth authorization URL in the user's default browser; failure is non-fatal — CLI continues and the URL is printed to stderr for manual use
